Name: ANDreW walLAcE
chief_complaint: Chest pain, shortness of breath, and sweating
hpi: 75-year-old male with history of diabetes and CAD presents with 6 hours of substernal chest pain, radiating to the left arm. Patient reports associated dyspnea and diaphoresis, rating pain 7/10. Symptoms are constant and not relieved by rest or aspirin.
physician_note: Patient presenting with acute chest pain concerning for acute coronary syndrome. Differential includes NSTEMI, unstable angina, esophageal spasm, or anxiety. Initial troponin is borderline elevated but requires serial monitoring. Plan includes serial EKGs, cardiac enzymes, CXR, IV access, oxygen, and cardiology consultation.

Name: MR. kYlE beRger
chief_complaint: Severe joint pain and swelling in my knee
hpi: Patient is a 21-year-old male with a history of arthritis presenting with acute onset severe right knee pain and swelling for the past 24 hours. He denies any recent trauma or injury. Pain is 8/10, constant, and associated with warmth and redness of the joint. He has not taken any new medications.
physician_note: A: 21 y/o M with known arthritis presenting with acute monoarticular knee pain, swelling, and erythema. Differential includes septic arthritis, gout flare, pseudogout, or exacerbation of underlying inflammatory arthritis. Given the acute presentation and localized inflammation, septic arthritis is a significant concern. P: Labs including CBC, ESR, CRP, uric acid, and synovial fluid analysis. Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics after joint aspiration. Consult Orthopedics for possible surgical washout. Analgesia and anti-inflammatory medications as appropriate.

Name: ryaN toRreS
chief_complaint: Chest pain and feeling generally unwell
hpi: A 64-year-old female with a history of type 2 diabetes and hyperlipidemia (on Lipitor) presents with substernal chest discomfort described as pressure, radiating to her left arm, for the past 4 hours. She also reports associated nausea and diaphoresis, but denies shortness of breath. Symptoms are intermittent and not relieved by rest.
physician_note: Assessment: 64F with T2DM and hyperlipidemia presenting with acute chest pain concerning for acute coronary syndrome (ACS). Differentials include unstable angina, NSTEMI, GERD, or musculoskeletal pain. Initial ECG shows non-specific ST changes. Plan: Initiate ACS pathway including serial troponins, aspirin, nitrates, and oxygen as needed. Monitor cardiac rhythm closely and consider cardiology consultation.

Name: erICa pATteRsOn
chief_complaint: Weakness, confusion, and stomach pain
hpi: A 22-year-old male with a history of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus presents to the ED complaining of generalized weakness, confusion, and diffuse abdominal pain for the past 12 hours. He reports poor oral intake and missed insulin doses due to nausea and malaise. His family notes increasing lethargy and difficulty rousing him over the last several hours.
physician_note: 22 y.o. male with T2DM presenting with symptoms highly suggestive of Diabetic Ketoacidosis. Differential diagnoses include DKA precipitated by infection, severe gastroenteritis, or metabolic encephalopathy. Plan: IV fluids, insulin drip initiation, serial chemistries, complete blood count, urinalysis, and blood cultures to rule out infectious etiology. Close monitoring of glucose and electrolytes.

Name: CASEy DEAn
chief_complaint: Severe asthma attack, trouble breathing
hpi: 15-year-old male with a history of asthma presents to the ED with acute onset of severe shortness of breath and wheezing, worsening over the past 4 hours. Patient reports using his albuterol inhaler every 2 hours without significant relief and states chest tightness. He denies fever or cough but reports taking ibuprofen yesterday for muscle aches.
physician_note: 15yo M presenting with severe asthma exacerbation. Patient is in moderate respiratory distress with diffuse expiratory wheezing noted bilaterally. Differential diagnoses include viral bronchitis, pneumonia, or foreign body aspiration. Plan includes nebulized albuterol/ipratropium, systemic corticosteroids, oxygen supplementation, and close monitoring of respiratory status.

Name: wENDY SMITH
chief_complaint: Black stools and feeling dizzy
hpi: 47-year-old female with a history of arthritis on daily aspirin presents with 3 days of dark, tarry stools and increasing dizziness and generalized weakness. Reports lightheadedness upon standing but no syncope. Denies fever, chest pain, or abdominal pain.
physician_note: A: Acute upper GI bleed likely secondary to chronic NSAID/Aspirin use, differential includes peptic ulcer disease, gastritis. P: Labs including CBC, CMP, Type & Screen, Lactate. IV fluids started. Consult GI for endoscopy. Monitor vital signs closely. Discontinue Aspirin.

Name: KYLe huMpHReY
chief_complaint: High blood sugar, feeling tired
hpi: A 47-year-old male with a history of Type 2 Diabetes presents to the ED complaining of generalized fatigue and increased thirst for the past three days. He reports his home glucose readings have been consistently elevated, ranging from 250-300 mg/dL. He denies any chest pain, shortness of breath, abdominal pain, or changes in mental status.
physician_note: Assessment: 47 y.o. male with known T2DM presenting with symptomatic hyperglycemia. Patient is alert and oriented, without signs of DKA or HHS. Differential includes medication non-adherence, dietary indiscretion, or underlying infection. Plan: Obtain full metabolic panel, A1c, and urinalysis. Administer subcutaneous insulin and oral rehydration. Provide diabetic education.

Name: WilliaM petersOn
chief_complaint: Severe belly pain and nausea
hpi: 31 y.o. male with obesity presents with acute onset severe right upper quadrant abdominal pain for the past 12 hours, radiating to the right shoulder. He reports associated nausea, one episode of non-bloody, non-bilious emesis, and subjective fever at home. Pain is constant, 8/10, unrelieved by oral paracetamol.
physician_note: Assessment: 31 y.o. obese male with acute RUQ pain concerning for acute cholecystitis. Differential includes biliary colic, pancreatitis, and gastritis. Plan: NPO, IV fluids, antiemetics, pain control, STAT CBC/CMP/Lipase/LFTs, abdominal ultrasound, and general surgery consult. Will monitor for signs of sepsis.

Name: marIA MartiN
chief_complaint: Fever and generalized weakness
hpi: A 58-year-old male with a history of cancer presents to the ED with a 2-day history of fever, chills, and generalized malaise. He reports temperatures up to 101.8F at home, partially relieved by Paracetamol. Patient is currently undergoing chemotherapy, with his last cycle 1 week ago. He denies any localized pain, cough, dysuria, or other focal symptoms.
physician_note: A&P: 58 y/o male with known cancer presenting with acute onset fever. Differential diagnosis includes chemotherapy-induced fever, viral illness, or early infection without a clear source. Given the patient's immunocompromised state, a comprehensive workup for potential infection is warranted. Plan: Obtain CBC, CMP, blood cultures, urinalysis, and a chest X-ray. Initiate empirical broad-spectrum antibiotics and IV fluids. Monitor vital signs closely in the ED.

Name: CHRistINa BARker
chief_complaint: Feeling very weak and tired
hpi: 83-year-old male with a history of metastatic prostate cancer presents with a 3-day history of progressive generalized weakness, fatigue, and reduced oral intake. He reports mild, diffuse body aches, somewhat alleviated by ibuprofen. Denies fever, chills, cough, chest pain, or any focal neurological deficits.
physician_note: A/P: 83 y/o male with metastatic prostate cancer presenting with generalized weakness. Differential includes dehydration, electrolyte imbalance, anemia, or potential progression of malignancy, though no acute red flags present. Patient is alert and oriented, vitals are stable. Plan: Initiate IV fluids, obtain CBC, CMP, and lactate. Monitor response to hydration, consider discharge vs. admission for further workup based on labs and clinical improvement.

scenario_hint: Outpatient diabetes review, well-controlled, no acute issues
chief_complaint: Routine diabetes check-up
hpi: A 52-year-old female with a history of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus presents for a routine outpatient review. She reports good glycemic control with home blood glucose monitoring, no episodes of hypoglycemia or hyperglycemia, and adheres to her insulin regimen. Patient denies any acute complaints such as chest pain, shortness of breath, fever, or altered mental status.
physician_note: Patient is a well-controlled diabetic presenting for routine follow-up. No acute medical issues identified during evaluation. Differential includes stable diabetes management. Plan is to continue current insulin regimen, reinforce dietary education, and encourage follow-up with her endocrinologist for ongoing care. No emergent interventions required.

Name: Lisa arcHEr
chief_complaint: Severe stomach pain and persistent nausea
hpi: 60-year-old female with a history of obesity presents with acute onset epigastric pain, described as a burning sensation, 7/10 in severity, radiating to her back. Pain started approximately 12 hours ago and is associated with nausea but no emesis. Patient reports chronic daily use of ibuprofen for knee pain for several months.
physician_note: Patient presenting with acute epigastric pain, likely gastritis or peptic ulcer disease exacerbated by NSAID use. Differential includes pancreatitis, cholecystitis, and cardiac etiology. Plan includes IV fluids, pantoprazole, antiemetics, EKG, and comprehensive lab panel to rule out other causes. Will monitor symptoms closely.

Name: jereMIAh YouNG
chief_complaint: Severe shortness of breath, asthma attack
hpi: Patient is an 18-year-old male with a history of asthma presenting with acute onset shortness of breath, wheezing, and chest tightness for the past 6 hours. He reports increasing reliance on his rescue inhaler, using it every 2-3 hours without significant relief. He denies fever, cough, or recent upper respiratory infection symptoms. His last asthma-related hospitalization was two years ago, and he is currently prescribed aspirin for an unknown indication.
physician_note: A&P: 18-year-old male with acute asthma exacerbation, likely triggered by environmental factors, presenting with respiratory distress and hypoxemia. Differential diagnoses include viral bronchitis, foreign body aspiration, or vocal cord dysfunction. Plan: Administer nebulized albuterol and ipratropium, systemic corticosteroids, and supplemental oxygen. Monitor respiratory status closely, and consider chest X-ray if no significant improvement or if atypical features develop. Further investigation into aspirin use is warranted.

Name: miCHAEL wILlIAMs
chief_complaint: Severe headache and blurry vision
hpi: A 17-year-old female with a history of hypertension presents with acute onset of the 'worst headache ever' for the past 4 hours, accompanied by blurry vision. She reports inconsistent compliance with her prescribed antihypertensive medication. She denies fever, neck stiffness, focal weakness, or recent trauma.
physician_note: A: 17yo F with known HTN presenting with severe headache and visual changes, concerning for hypertensive emergency vs. complicated migraine vs. PRES. P: Will admit for close BP monitoring and urgent workup. Order STAT head CT, labs including CBC, CHEM, and urinalysis. Initiate IV antihypertensives as needed and consult neurology.

Name: KevIN milLer
chief_complaint: Severe right knee pain with fever
hpi: A 35-year-old male with a history of generalized arthritis presents with acute onset of worsening right knee pain, swelling, and erythema for the past 2 days. He reports associated subjective fevers, chills, and generalized malaise, refractory to his usual paracetamol. No recent trauma or skin breaks noted on the affected limb. Patient denies any other new or concerning symptoms.
physician_note: A: 35M presenting with acute monoarticular arthritis, likely inflammatory or infectious etiology. Differential diagnoses include septic arthritis, acute gout flare, or an inflammatory arthritis exacerbation. Clinical picture suggests a systemic inflammatory response. P: Labs sent including CBC, ESR, CRP, lactate, and synovial fluid aspiration for analysis. Empiric broad-spectrum antibiotics initiated post-cultures. Pain management with NSAIDs (if appropriate) and orthopedic surgery consult for evaluation and potential washout. Close monitoring for signs of sepsis.

Name: KelLIE FrAnKlin
chief_complaint: Can't catch my breath, asthma acting up
hpi: 65-year-old female with a history of asthma presents to the ED with acute onset of progressively worsening shortness of breath, wheezing, and chest tightness for the past 6 hours. She reports using her albuterol inhaler every 2 hours with only transient relief. Denies fever, cough, or recent illness.
physician_note: A: Acute asthma exacerbation. Differential includes bronchitis or early pneumonia. P: Administer albuterol/ipratropium nebulizer, oral corticosteroids (prednisone), and monitor respiratory status. Will reassess after initial treatments and consider chest X-ray if no improvement.

Name: maRy FLemING
chief_complaint: Fever, chills, and feeling weak for a day
hpi: A 44-year-old male with a history of colon cancer, currently undergoing chemotherapy, presents with a 24-hour history of subjective fever, chills, and generalized weakness. He reports no cough, dysuria, or focal pain. His last chemotherapy cycle was 5 days ago, and he is concerned about infection.
physician_note: A&P: 44 y/o male with history of colon cancer on active chemotherapy presenting with neutropenic fever. Differential includes bacterial sepsis, viral infection, or tumor fever. Initial workup to include pan-cultures, CBC with differential, CMP, lactate, and urinalysis. Plan to initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics (e.g., cefepime or meropenem) after cultures drawn and consider empiric antifungal coverage if no improvement. Admission for close monitoring and further management.

Name: NaNcY GonZaLes
chief_complaint: Severe belly pain, abnormal test result
hpi: A 44-year-old obese female presents to the ED with acute onset, severe right upper quadrant abdominal pain for the past 24 hours. The pain is constant, rates 8/10, and radiates to her back, exacerbated by movement. She reports associated nausea and one episode of non-bloody, non-bilious emesis. She recently underwent an outpatient workup for chronic dyspepsia, and an abdominal ultrasound performed yesterday was reported as "abnormal," prompting this ED visit. She has been taking ibuprofen with minimal relief.
physician_note: Assessment: 44F presenting with acute, severe RUQ pain, nausea, and vomiting, with a recent abnormal outpatient abdominal ultrasound. Differential diagnosis includes acute cholecystitis, choledocholithiasis, and less likely, pancreatitis or hepatitis. Given her clinical picture and obesity, acute cholecystitis is highly suspected. Plan: Initiate IVF, antiemetics, and broad-spectrum antibiotics. Obtain emergent repeat abdominal ultrasound and consult general surgery for potential cholecystectomy. Labs: CBC, CMP, lipase, LFTs.

Name: daVID kLEIn
chief_complaint: Sudden, severe knee pain and swelling
hpi: 17-year-old female with history of juvenile idiopathic arthritis (JIA) presents with 2-day history of acute onset severe right knee pain and swelling, worse with movement. She denies any recent trauma but reports subjective fevers at home. Patient has been taking prescribed paracetamol without significant relief.
physician_note: A: 17yo F with JIA presenting with acute monoarticular arthritis, differential includes septic arthritis vs. acute inflammatory flare. P: Labs (CBC, ESR, CRP), X-ray right knee, orthopedic consult for possible arthrocentesis. IV fluids and pain control as needed pending further workup.

Name: jaSON roMerO
chief_complaint: Worsening foot pain and redness
hpi: 26F with T1DM presents with a 3-day history of worsening left foot pain and redness. She notes a small cut on her toe developed a week ago which has progressively become more painful and swollen. Denies fever or chills but reports feeling generally unwell.
physician_note: A&P: 26F with T1DM presenting with presumed cellulitis of the left foot. Vitals are stable, and labs show mild hyperglycemia but no significant leukocytosis. Differential includes osteomyelitis, gout, or superficial thrombophlebitis. Plan: IV antibiotics (e.g., vancomycin, zosyn), elevate foot, wound care, and consult podiatry for further evaluation. Monitor glucose levels closely.

Name: MIChAel mILLEr
chief_complaint: Weakness, confusion, and not acting right
hpi: 82-year-old obese female presents to the ED with a two-day history of progressive weakness, lethargy, and confusion. Her family reports she has not been herself, is disoriented to place and time, and has had decreased oral intake. She denies chest pain, shortness of breath, or focal neurological deficits, but has been complaining of some dysuria.
physician_note: A: Elderly obese female with acute altered mental status, likely secondary to urosepsis given dysuria and lethargy. Differential includes metabolic encephalopathy, stroke, or other infectious etiologies. P: Labs including CBC, CMP, urinalysis with culture, lactate, troponin. IV fluids, broad-spectrum antibiotics after cultures, and close monitoring.

Name: mElIsSA FlEtchER
chief_complaint: Feeling very weak and lightheaded
hpi: 54-year-old female with a history of type 2 diabetes mellitus and hyperlipidemia on Lipitor presents with generalized weakness and lightheadedness for the past 12 hours. She denies chest pain, shortness of breath, or focal neurological deficits. Patient reports inconsistent blood glucose monitoring recently.
physician_note: A: 54yo female with T2DM presenting with generalized weakness, likely multifactorial including potential for suboptimal glycemic control or dehydration. DDx includes hypoglycemia, hyperglycemia, orthostatic hypotension, or mild infection. P: Will obtain comprehensive metabolic panel, CBC, and urinalysis. Administer 1L IV normal saline. Monitor blood glucose and vital signs closely. If labs are reassuring, consider discharge with strict return precautions and diabetes education.

scenario_hint: Hypertensive urgency: BP 195/115, asymptomatic, no end-organ findings
chief_complaint: Extremely high blood pressure reading
hpi: A 58-year-old female with a history of hypertension, currently on Lipitor, presents to the emergency department after a home blood pressure reading of 195/115 mmHg. Patient denies any associated symptoms including headache, chest pain, shortness of breath, vision changes, or neurological deficits. She reports adherence to her current medication regimen.
physician_note: 58F with known essential hypertension presenting with asymptomatic hypertensive urgency. Physical exam reveals no signs of acute end-organ damage. Differential diagnosis includes medication non-adherence, undiagnosed secondary hypertension, or exacerbation of essential hypertension. Plan is to initiate oral antihypertensive therapy, observe for blood pressure reduction, obtain basic labs, and arrange for close follow-up with her primary care physician.

scenario_hint: Routine diabetes follow-up, A1c at goal, no acute findings
chief_complaint: Routine diabetes check-up
hpi: 44-year-old female with a history of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, well-controlled on insulin, presents for a routine follow-up visit. Patient reports no acute complaints, denies any symptoms of hypo or hyperglycemia, chest pain, shortness of breath, or fever. Her last A1c was reported to be at goal.
physician_note: Assessment: 44F with well-controlled Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, presenting for routine follow-up with no acute concerns. Differential diagnoses include stable T2DM. Plan: Continue current insulin regimen and reinforce dietary and exercise recommendations. Patient advised to follow up with her primary care physician and endocrinologist for ongoing management. No acute emergency department interventions are required at this time.

Name: BrOOKe RhoDeS
chief_complaint: Bad asthma attack, can't breathe
hpi: An 81-year-old female with a known history of asthma presents to the emergency department reporting severe shortness of breath and wheezing which started approximately 6 hours prior to arrival. She states her home albuterol inhaler has provided minimal relief, and her breathing has progressively worsened. The patient denies any associated fever, cough, or chest pain.
physician_note: This 81F presents with an acute asthma exacerbation, likely moderate in severity. Differential diagnosis includes acute bronchitis, COPD exacerbation, and early pneumonia, though physical exam and initial labs are most consistent with asthma. Plan includes aggressive bronchodilator therapy, systemic corticosteroids, and respiratory support as needed.

Name: eRIn gARCIa
chief_complaint: Severe weakness, chills, and fever
hpi: A 64-year-old male with a history of metastatic colon cancer, currently undergoing chemotherapy, presents to the ED with a 24-hour history of severe generalized weakness, subjective fever, and chills. He denies any focal symptoms such as cough, dysuria, or headache. His last chemotherapy infusion was one week prior.
physician_note: This patient presents with suspected febrile neutropenia in the context of recent chemotherapy. Differential diagnoses include sepsis, chemotherapy-induced myelosuppression, or a viral syndrome. Initial management will focus on pan-culturing, immediate broad-spectrum antibiotics, aggressive fluid resuscitation, and close monitoring for signs of septic shock.

Name: jamES BasS phD
chief_complaint: Trouble breathing and wheezing
hpi: 13-year-old male with a history of asthma presents with acute onset of shortness of breath and wheezing for the past 12 hours. Symptoms worsened after playing outside, and his albuterol inhaler provided minimal relief. Patient reports also taking ibuprofen earlier for general body aches, which have since resolved.
physician_note: Acutely ill-appearing adolescent male in moderate respiratory distress, consistent with severe asthma exacerbation. Differential includes viral bronchiolitis or early pneumonia. Plan involves immediate nebulized bronchodilators, systemic corticosteroids, close respiratory monitoring, and further workup as indicated by response to initial treatment.

Name: roBERT tHoMpsoN
chief_complaint: Bad stomach ache, right side
hpi: A 21-year-old obese male presents with acute onset, severe right upper quadrant abdominal pain for the past 8 hours. The pain is constant, sharp, and radiates to his right shoulder. He reports associated nausea, anorexia, and a single episode of non-bloody emesis. He has taken oral paracetamol without any significant relief.
physician_note: A: Acute cholecystitis, given RUQ pain, nausea, and obesity. Other considerations include gastritis or peptic ulcer disease. P: NPO, IV fluids, analgesia, and antiemetics. Order CBC, LFTs, lipase, and an RUQ ultrasound. Surgical consult pending imaging results.

Name: joy CHAveZ
chief_complaint: Chest pressure and lightheadedness
hpi: A 47-year-old female with a history of hypertension and daily aspirin use presents with 6 hours of intermittent, dull substernal chest pressure radiating to her left arm, accompanied by lightheadedness. She denies significant dyspnea, diaphoresis, or nausea. Symptoms are not relieved by rest and are mildly exacerbated by exertion. She reports no recent trauma or fever.
physician_note: A&P: 47F presenting with chest pain and lightheadedness, concerning for acute coronary syndrome given risk factors and symptoms. Differential includes unstable angina, NSTEMI, and non-cardiac etiologies such as GERD or anxiety. Initial workup including ECG showed non-specific T-wave changes. Plan: Continue cardiac monitoring, administer aspirin 325mg chewable, draw serial troponins and other cardiac enzymes, and obtain a chest X-ray. Will admit for further evaluation and management.

Name: RIChArD LaWSon
chief_complaint: Severe chest pain and can't catch my breath.
hpi: A 78-year-old obese female with a history of hypertension and hyperlipidemia, currently on Aspirin, presents to the ED reporting sudden onset substernal chest pain for the past 2 hours. She describes the pain as a crushing sensation radiating to her left arm, rated 8/10, and reports associated severe shortness of breath and diaphoresis. The pain is persistent and not relieved by rest or her home medications. She denies recent trauma or fever.
physician_note: 78F presenting with acute onset crushing chest pain and dyspnea, concerning for acute coronary syndrome (ACS), pulmonary embolism (PE), or severe decompensated heart failure. Initial ECG shows non-specific T-wave inversions, and initial troponin is borderline elevated. Plan includes serial cardiac enzymes, D-dimer, CXR, and emergent cardiology consult. Patient will be admitted for further workup and continuous cardiac monitoring. Will also consider a CT angiogram of the chest if PE risk remains high.

Name: deReK VALDEz
chief_complaint: Fever, chills, and extreme weakness
hpi: A 44-year-old male with a history of lymphoma, currently undergoing chemotherapy, presents to the ED with a 2-day history of subjective fever, chills, and profound generalized weakness. He reports no cough, dysuria, or focal pain. Patient denies recent travel or sick contacts.
physician_note: 44M with known lymphoma on active chemotherapy presenting with febrile neutropenia. Differential includes sepsis, pneumonia, or other opportunistic infection. Plan includes pan-cultures (blood, urine), broad-spectrum IV antibiotics (e.g., cefepime/vancomycin), IV fluids for support, and admission for close monitoring and further workup.

Name: cLareNCE evaNs
chief_complaint: Severe shortness of breath, asthma attack
hpi: 75-year-old female with a history of asthma presents with acute onset of worsening dyspnea and wheezing over the last 12 hours. Symptoms are unrelieved by her home albuterol inhaler. Patient reports chest tightness and increased work of breathing. She denies fever, cough, or recent illness.
physician_note: A&P: 75F with acute asthma exacerbation. Differential includes CHF, pneumonia, and cardiac ischemia given her age and aspirin use. Plan: Nebulized bronchodilators, systemic corticosteroids, oxygen therapy, EKG, CXR, and labs including cardiac enzymes to rule out other etiologies. Close monitoring for respiratory decompensation.

scenario_hint: Intentional acetaminophen overdose 30 tabs 4h ago, suicidal ideation
chief_complaint: Took too many Tylenol pills
hpi: A 34-year-old female presents to the emergency department after intentionally ingesting approximately 30 tablets of 500mg acetaminophen (total 15g) four hours prior to arrival, reportedly due to suicidal ideation. She denies other co-ingestants or alcohol use. Patient has a history of asthma but denies current respiratory distress, and is currently asymptomatic but admits to feeling depressed.
physician_note: A: 34F with acute acetaminophen overdose, potential for acute liver injury. Suicidal ideation is a significant concern requiring immediate psychiatric evaluation. Differential includes other toxic ingestions or underlying medical conditions contributing to symptoms. P: Initiate N-acetylcysteine protocol (NAC) per nomogram immediately. Obtain serum acetaminophen level, LFTs, INR, glucose, and electrolytes. Consult psychiatry for evaluation of suicidal ideation and disposition planning. Monitor vital signs and clinical status closely.

scenario_hint: STEMI: tearing chest pain + nausea + cold sweats
chief_complaint: Severe tearing chest pain with nausea and sweats
hpi: A 62-year-old male with a history of hypertension and on daily aspirin presents with acute onset severe, tearing chest pain radiating to his left arm, starting approximately 2 hours ago. He reports associated nausea, profuse diaphoresis, and shortness of breath. The pain is unrelenting and rated 9/10 in intensity.
physician_note: Assessment: 62M presenting with acute chest pain highly concerning for STEMI given the tearing quality, radiation, and associated symptoms including diaphoresis. Differential includes aortic dissection, pulmonary embolism, and acute pericarditis. Plan: Immediate ECG, cardiac enzymes, IV access, oxygen, aspirin (if not already taken), nitrates if BP allows, and activate cath lab given high suspicion.

Name: EmILY sImmoNS
chief_complaint: Bad knee pain and feeling sick
hpi: This 15-year-old male with a history of juvenile idiopathic arthritis, currently on aspirin, presents with acute onset of severe right knee pain, swelling, and subjective fever for the past 24 hours. He reports generalized malaise and inability to bear weight. Denies any recent trauma or sick contacts. He has not been able to sleep due to pain.
physician_note: Patient presents with acute monoarticular arthritis and systemic symptoms concerning for septic arthritis. Differential includes acute JIA flare, reactive arthritis, or crystalline arthropathy. Plan includes immediate arthrocentesis for synovial fluid analysis, blood cultures, and initiation of empiric IV antibiotics (e.g., vancomycin and ceftriaxone) after cultures are drawn. Pain control with IV analgesia will be provided. Close monitoring of vitals and serial exams are essential.

Name: DianE jImeNEz
chief_complaint: Severe chest pain, feeling short of breath
hpi: 56 y.o. male with history of Type 2 Diabetes presents to the ED with sudden onset substernal chest pain for the past 2 hours. Pain is described as a crushing pressure, radiating to his left arm, and is associated with diaphoresis and mild shortness of breath. He denies recent trauma or fever.
physician_note: 56 y.o. diabetic male presenting with classic anginal symptoms concerning for acute coronary syndrome. Differential includes NSTEMI, unstable angina, GERD, and pericarditis. Plan is to admit to observation, obtain serial troponins and ECGs, start aspirin, nitrates, and beta-blockers, and consult cardiology for further management.

Name: AarOn walteR
chief_complaint: Worsening joint pain and fatigue
hpi: This 15-year-old female with a history of juvenile idiopathic arthritis and on aspirin presents with a 3-day history of worsening polyarticular joint pain, primarily in her knees and wrists. She reports associated fatigue and intermittent low-grade fevers, with a subjective decrease in appetite. No new trauma or recent infections are reported.
physician_note: A&P: 15 y/o F with known JIA presenting with an acute exacerbation. Vitals are stable, but the patient exhibits signs of discomfort and generalized malaise. Differential diagnosis includes JIA flare, viral arthralgia, or potential medication-related side effects. Plan: Order CBC, ESR, CRP, and a comprehensive metabolic panel to assess for systemic inflammation and organ function. Initiate pain management and consider adjusting her current medication regimen after further evaluation.

Name: Neil Black
chief_complaint: Severe stomach ache for two days
hpi: A 43-year-old female with a history of type 2 diabetes presents with 2 days of worsening diffuse abdominal pain, described as cramping and intermittent. She denies fever, nausea, vomiting, or changes in bowel habits. The patient reports taking ibuprofen without relief prior to arrival.
physician_note: Patient presents with acute abdominal pain, likely non-surgical given benign abdominal exam and largely normal initial laboratory results. Differential diagnoses include diabetic gastroparesis, gastroenteritis, or atypical urinary tract infection. Plan is for IV fluid resuscitation, symptomatic management with antiemetics as needed, comprehensive metabolic panel, urinalysis, and close observation in the emergency department.

Name: CHrisTOPhEr mArTIneZ
chief_complaint: Severe shortness of breath and wheezing
hpi: 41-year-old male with a history of asthma presents to the ED complaining of progressively worsening shortness of breath and wheezing for the past 24 hours. He reports increased cough productive of clear sputum and has used his albuterol inhaler every 2-3 hours without significant relief. He denies fever or chills but feels very anxious due to his breathing difficulty.
physician_note: A: Acute severe asthma exacerbation, likely triggered by a viral upper respiratory infection. Differential includes pneumonia, acute bronchitis, or anaphylaxis. P: Initiate continuous nebulized albuterol and ipratropium, systemic corticosteroids (prednisone PO), supplemental oxygen to maintain SpO2 >92%. Monitor respiratory status closely, consider chest X-ray to rule out pneumonia if no improvement. Admission likely if no rapid response to treatment.

Name: whitNEy DAViS
chief_complaint: Weakness and lightheadedness for two days
hpi: 38-year-old male with a history of Diabetes Mellitus presents with two days of generalized weakness and lightheadedness. Patient denies chest pain, shortness of breath, fever, or focal neurological deficits. He reports feeling fatigued and has had a decreased oral intake recently, attributing it to increased work stress. He states he has been compliant with his diabetic medications and daily aspirin.
physician_note: A: Diabetic patient presenting with generalized weakness and lightheadedness, likely secondary to mild dehydration or poorly controlled blood glucose, though symptoms are nonspecific. Differential includes viral illness or mild electrolyte imbalance. P: IV fluids administered, point-of-care glucose obtained, and comprehensive metabolic panel drawn. Patient will be monitored for symptom improvement and lab results. Discharge planning will include dietary counseling and emphasis on regular fluid intake.

Name: Manuel roGErS
chief_complaint: Shortness of breath and wheezing for two days
hpi: A 73-year-old female with a history of asthma presents with increased shortness of breath, wheezing, and cough for the past two days. She reports using her albuterol inhaler more frequently with diminishing relief. Denies fever, chest pain, or recent illness contact.
physician_note: 73F with acute asthma exacerbation. Differential includes viral bronchitis, early pneumonia, CHF exacerbation. Plan: Nebulized albuterol/ipratropium, oral steroids, chest X-ray, labs, and monitor respiratory status. Will consider admission if no significant improvement.

scenario_hint: Rheumatoid arthritis flare, joint pain, no infectious signs
chief_complaint: Severe joint pain, arthritis flare-up
hpi: 67-year-old female presents with a 3-day history of worsening polyarticular joint pain, primarily affecting her hands, wrists, and knees. She reports stiffness, swelling, and tenderness, which is significantly interfering with her activities of daily living. Name: KIMBerly wILLIams

Name: tODD FisHER

Differential includes severe DKA precipitated by non-compliance, infection, or pump malfunction. Name: KAthRyN fReNCH

Name: bENJAMin rIch

Will monitor for signs of systemic infection while awaiting synovial fluid results.

Name: mark jACKSON

physician_note: A&P: 18M with known hypertension presenting with acute onset severe headache and visual changes concerning for hypertensive emergency with potential end-organ damage. Differential includes severe migraine, posterior reversible encephalopathy syndrome (PRES), or intracranial hemorrhage. Plan: Obtain stat head CT, initiate IV labetalol for blood pressure control, draw CBC, CHEM-7, troponin, and urinalysis. Continuous cardiac monitoring and neuro checks are indicated. Consult neurology and cardiology for further management.

Name: cHaRLes mASON

hpi: 31-year-old female with a history of asthma presents to the ED with acute onset of severe shortness of breath, cough, and wheezing for the past 6 hours. physician_note: A: Acute asthma exacerbation, likely triggered by environmental allergens. Differential includes viral bronchitis, allergic reaction, or early pneumonia. scenario_hint: Massive PE: syncope, hypotension, RV strain, tPA considered

Plan includes immediate tPA administration, initiation of heparin drip, stat CT pulmonary angiography, and critical care consultation for further management.

Name: Jesse hAnCOcK

physician_note: A&P: 76 y/o female with type 2 diabetes presenting with altered mental status, fever, and signs of systemic inflammatory response. Plan: Initiate aggressive IV fluid resuscitation, obtain STAT labs including CBC, CMP, lactate, blood cultures, and urinalysis, and start broad-spectrum antibiotics pending culture results. Patient will be admitted to the ICU for close monitoring and further management.

Name: amANda BEcK

physician_note: A: 23 y/o F with ALL on chemo presenting with presumed febrile neutropenia. Differential includes early sepsis of unknown origin, drug-induced fever, or viral syndrome. Name: jAsoN leOnARd

physician_note: Assessment: Acute severe asthma exacerbation in a 48-year-old male. Differential diagnoses include acute bronchitis, pneumonia, and atypical cardiac event given age and Aspirin use. Name: HuNTEr vEgA

Name: natashA SMITh

Name: RaChel AdKinS

Name: scoTt adams

Name: wILLIAM hIlL

Name: MIchaEl garcIa

Name: MEgAN mUnOZ

Name: StePHanIE mCdOnAlD

Administer aspirin, nitroglycerin, and consider heparin drip if subsequent troponins rise; consult cardiology for further evaluation.

Name: jasminE frenCh

physician_note: A&P: 61M with colon cancer on chemo presenting with neutropenic fever and acute abdominal pain. High concern for typhlitis, sepsis, or C. difficile colitis. Name: jErEmY tUrnEr

Name: jaCquELINe PAtTERSON

Name: PATrick RobBInS

physician_note: A 57 y/o male with hx of HTN presenting with acute thunderclap headache. Differential diagnosis includes subarachnoid hemorrhage, reversible cerebral vasoconstriction syndrome, and hypertensive urgency. Patient is neurologically intact on exam. Plan includes STAT head CT without contrast, comprehensive metabolic panel, CBC, and close monitoring of blood pressure. Pain management initiated.

Name: dANIeLLe MOReno

Differential includes severe dehydration, acute infection, or medication non-compliance.

Name: JONathAn manNING

Name: jEROmE DAY

physician_note: Assessment: Acute exacerbation of right knee osteoarthritis. Discharge home with pain management and follow-up with orthopedics as an outpatient.

Name: Samuel mooRE

Differential includes acute arthritis flare, septic arthritis (less likely given no systemic symptoms or inflammatory markers), or crystal-induced arthropathy. Plan includes NSAIDs, multimodal pain control, and referral to rheumatology for follow-up.

Name: MEGAn gUZMAN

Differential includes DKA/HHS, sepsis, or CVA. Initial labs show severe hyperglycemia, leukocytosis, and elevated lactate. Plan is aggressive IV fluids, insulin drip, broad-spectrum antibiotics after cultures, and head CT to rule out acute neurological event.

Name: jusTiN BridGEs

Name: LInda hoLT

Name: CaRLa JenSeN
chief_complaint: Worsening shortness of breath and wheezing
hpi: 81 y/o male with known history of asthma presents to the ED with progressively worsening shortness of breath and wheezing over the last 48 hours. Symptoms are exacerbated by activity and mildly improved with rest.

Differential diagnoses include congestive heart failure and pneumonia, though less likely given the patient's history and lack of typical symptoms. Plan: Initiate nebulized albuterol and ipratropium, administer systemic corticosteroids, and supplemental oxygen to maintain SpO2 >92%. Obtain chest X-ray to rule out consolidation and consider empiric antibiotics if signs of infection develop. Monitor respiratory status closely.

Name: NAThan Walker

Will consider discharge with close follow-up if stable.

Name: MaRIE wilLIAms

Primary differential is DKA, consider appendicitis or severe gastroenteritis given abdominal pain. Plan: IV fluids, insulin drip initiation, serial chemistries, EKG, and close monitoring in ED/ICU.

Name: TYleR sWEEnEy

Name: JeffREY SMITH

scenario_hint: Anaphylactic shock to penicillin: hives, angioedema, hypotension, wheezing, given IM epi

P: Continue aggressive management for anaphylaxis including IV fluids, steroids, H1/H2 blockers, and close monitoring. Prepare for potential intubation. Admit to ICU for further management.

Name: jAMES GARCiA

physician_note: A/P: 53F with symptoms concerning for acute upper GI bleed, likely NSAID-induced gastritis or ulcer given ibuprofen use and melena. Consult GI for possible urgent endoscopy and admit patient for further workup and close observation.

Name: DebORAH CoLliER

Differential diagnoses include severe gastroenteritis, other metabolic derangements.

Name: noAh RObErTS

Name: TEResa roSE

hpi: A 44-year-old female with a history of cancer presents to the ED with acute onset dyspnea and sharp, pleuritic chest pain that started an hour ago. She reports no fever or cough. Pain is 8/10 and worsens with deep inspiration, unrelieved by her home paracetamol.
physician_note: Patient is a 44F with known cancer presenting with acute pleuritic chest pain and dyspnea. Initial workup concerning for pulmonary embolism given her history and symptoms. Differential includes PE, pneumonia, pericarditis, musculoskeletal pain. Plan to admit for further evaluation, initiate anticoagulation, and obtain CT pulmonary angiogram.

Name: jEAn gRavES
chief_complaint: Generalized weakness and fatigue
hpi: 80-year-old male with a history of hypertension presents with a chief complaint of generalized weakness and fatigue for the past 2 days. He denies any chest pain, shortness of breath, fever, or chills. Patient is scheduled for an elective cataract surgery tomorrow and reports feeling more tired than usual which prompted this visit. His hypertension is reportedly well-controlled with home medications.
physician_note: A: Generalized weakness, likely multifactorial. Differentials include mild dehydration, viral syndrome, or mild exacerbation of chronic conditions. P: Initiate IV fluids for hydration, obtain routine labs including CBC, CMP, and EKG. Observe for clinical improvement and re-evaluate prior to discharge, potentially rescheduling elective surgery if symptoms persist.

scenario_hint: Metastatic breast cancer with severe bone pain refractory to home opioids
chief_complaint: Unbearable bone pain, cannot get relief
hpi: A 55-year-old female with a history of metastatic breast cancer presents to the ED with severe, unremitting bone pain for the past 24 hours. The pain, localized to her lower back and right hip, is rated 10/10 and is not improving with her prescribed oral opioids or ibuprofen. She reports increased weakness and difficulty ambulating, raising concern for pathological fracture.
physician_note: Patient is a 55F with metastatic breast cancer presenting with acute on chronic severe bone pain concerning for pathological fracture or impending spinal cord compression. Initial assessment reveals significant pain and mild neurological deficit in her right leg. Plan includes IV pain management, stat imaging (X-ray/MRI spine/pelvis), and oncology consultation for further management and potential radiation therapy. Will monitor neurological status closely.

Name: nATHaN marTIn
chief_complaint: Worst headache ever, blurry vision
hpi: A 26-year-old female with a history of hypertension and on aspirin presents to the ED with a severe, throbbing headache for the past 12 hours, accompanied by new-onset blurry vision. She reports non-compliance with her home antihypertensive medications for the last week. Denies fever, focal weakness, or neck stiffness.
physician_note: Assessment: 26 y.o. female with hypertensive urgency presenting with headache and visual changes. Differential diagnoses include pre-eclampsia (even if not currently pregnant, given age and HTN history), posterior reversible encephalopathy syndrome (PRES), complicated migraine, or subarachnoid hemorrhage. Plan: Initiate IV antihypertensive therapy (Labetalol), stat head CT without contrast, EKG, and comprehensive lab panel. Strict BP control and neurology consult pending imaging results.

Name: cArlY acosta
chief_complaint: High blood sugar, very thirsty and tired
hpi: A 14-year-old female with a history of Type 1 Diabetes presents to the ED with complaints of increased thirst, frequent urination, and generalized fatigue for the past 24 hours. She reports her home blood glucose readings have been consistently elevated, often above 250 mg/dL, despite extra insulin doses. She denies vomiting, abdominal pain, or shortness of breath. No recent infections or changes in diet reported.
physician_note: Assessment: 14 y/o female with T1DM presenting with symptomatic hyperglycemia. Differential includes non-compliance with insulin regimen vs. intercurrent illness contributing to insulin resistance. Plan: Obtain STAT labs including CBC, BMP, A1c, and urinalysis. Administer subcutaneous insulin and IV fluids. Educate on sick day rules and consider endocrinology consult for medication adjustment prior to discharge.

Name: JeSsiCa aDaMS
chief_complaint: Increased thirst, fatigue, and abdominal pain
hpi: A 14-year-old female with a history of Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus presents with a 3-day history of polyuria, polydipsia, and generalized fatigue. She reports poor appetite and intermittent diffuse abdominal discomfort. Patient admits to inconsistent insulin use over the past week due to feeling unwell, with home blood glucose readings consistently elevated.
physician_note: Assessment: 14 y/o female with T1DM presenting with symptoms concerning for diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA) vs. severe hyperglycemia. Differential includes acute gastroenteritis or urinary tract infection. Plan: Order STAT labs (BMP, CBC, VBG, urinalysis). Initiate IV fluid resuscitation. Prepare for insulin drip protocol if DKA confirmed and consult endocrinology.

Name: EtHaN RobINSON
chief_complaint: Severe abdominal pain, fever, and generalized weakness
hpi: 84-year-old male with history of pancreatic cancer s/p recent chemotherapy presents with 6 hours of worsening, diffuse abdominal pain, associated with chills, subjective fever, and generalized weakness. Denies vomiting or diarrhea. Last chemotherapy cycle was 10 days ago, raising concern for neutropenic fever or acute abdominal process.
physician_note: Elderly male with active cancer and recent chemotherapy presenting with signs of systemic inflammatory response and acute abdomen. Differential includes neutropenic fever with occult infection, bowel perforation, or tumor-related complication. Patient appears acutely ill and requires immediate workup and intervention. Plan includes STAT labs, pan-CT abdomen/pelvis, broad-spectrum antibiotics, IV fluids, and admission to inpatient oncology for further management.

Name: RIChaRd VeLasquEZ
chief_complaint: Feeling weak and dizzy for several days
hpi: 81-year-old obese male presents to the ED with a chief complaint of generalized weakness and lightheadedness for the past 3 days. He reports decreased appetite and occasional presyncopal episodes when standing up quickly. No associated chest pain, shortness of breath, fever, or focal neurological deficits. Patient denies recent illness or trauma.
physician_note: Assessment: Elderly obese male presenting with acute generalized weakness and lightheadedness, likely multifactorial. Differential diagnoses include dehydration, electrolyte derangement, cardiac etiology (e.g., arrhythmia), or early infection. Plan: Initiate IV fluids, obtain comprehensive metabolic panel, CBC, EKG, and cardiac enzymes. Will monitor vital signs and consider further imaging if no clear etiology found.

Name: chRisTOPHEr LIttlE
chief_complaint: Diabetic feeling sick, fever, confused
hpi: Patient is a 51-year-old male with a history of Type 2 Diabetes presenting with 3 days of worsening malaise, nausea, and vomiting. He reports subjective fevers, polyuria, and decreased oral intake. His wife notes he has been more confused and lethargic today.
physician_note: Assessment: 51 y.o. male with T2DM presenting with likely severe sepsis given fever, altered mental status, and abnormal labs (leukocytosis, hyperglycemia, elevated lactate). Differential includes DKA/HHS, acute kidney injury, or other severe infectious etiologies. Plan: Aggressive IV fluid resuscitation, broad-spectrum antibiotics after cultures, obtain UA, chest X-ray, replete electrolytes, consider insulin drip for hyperglycemia. Admit to critical care for close monitoring.

Name: sTephanie SChULTZ
chief_complaint: Teenager feeling very weak, dizzy, and sick to stomach
hpi: 15 y/o female with Type 1 Diabetes presents with 3 days of polyuria, polydipsia, and fatigue. She reports increasing nausea and diffuse abdominal pain for the last 24 hours, with decreased oral intake and occasional vomiting. Her parents note she has been more lethargic and irritable today. She denies fever or recent illness prior to symptom onset.
physician_note: Assessment: 15 y/o female with T1DM presenting with symptoms concerning for diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA) vs. severe hyperglycemia with dehydration, possibly triggered by an infection. Differential also includes acute gastroenteritis or appendicitis given abdominal pain. Plan: Initiate DKA protocol, including IV fluids and insulin drip. Obtain comprehensive metabolic panel, CBC, urinalysis, and blood cultures. Monitor closely for neurological changes and electrolyte imbalances.

Name: jAMES huRst DvM
chief_complaint: Chest pain and feeling weak
hpi: A 55-year-old male with a history of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus and hyperlipidemia presents to the ED with substernal chest pressure radiating to his left arm, onset approximately 2 hours ago. He describes the pain as a dull ache, 7/10 in severity, associated with diaphoresis and mild dyspnea. He denies fever, cough, or recent trauma. His home medication includes Lipitor.
physician_note: A&P: 55 y.o. male presenting with acute onset chest pain concerning for acute coronary syndrome (ACS). Given his cardiac risk factors (DM, hyperlipidemia), ACS must be ruled out immediately. Differentials include esophageal spasm, pericarditis, or musculoskeletal pain. Plan: STAT EKG, cardiac enzymes, IV access, aspirin, nitrates if BP allows, and oxygen as needed. Consult Cardiology if troponins are elevated.

Name: MARY ColEmaN
chief_complaint: Sudden dizziness and feeling weak
hpi: A 66-year-old obese female with a history of hyperlipidemia on Lipitor presents with acute onset dizziness and generalized weakness starting this morning. Symptoms are worse with standing and improve with rest. Denies chest pain, palpitations, focal neurological deficits, or recent fevers.
physician_note: Patient presents with acute dizziness and weakness concerning for orthostatic changes, possible transient ischemic attack, or medication effect. Vitals are stable, and initial physical exam is non-focal. Plan includes comprehensive metabolic panel, EKG, head CT if indicated by further exam, and IV fluid hydration.

Name: laCeY sANdOVAL
chief_complaint: Feeling weak and confused for two days
hpi: A 78-year-old female with a history of diabetes presents to the ED with generalized weakness and confusion for the past two days. Family reports she has been progressively less interactive, refusing food, and had a witnessed fall at home this morning without significant injury. The patient takes ibuprofen occasionally for chronic arthritis pain.
physician_note: Assessment: 78 y/o female with diabetes presenting with acute altered mental status and generalized weakness. Differential includes severe hyperglycemia (HHS vs DKA), urinary tract infection, sepsis, or medication-induced encephalopathy. Plan: STAT labs including CBC, CMP, UA, lactate, and blood cultures. Begin aggressive IV fluid resuscitation and obtain a 12-lead EKG. Consult internal medicine for urgent management.

Name: sharON snYDeR
chief_complaint: Feeling tired and a bit lightheaded
hpi: A 74-year-old female with a history of type 2 diabetes and hyperlipidemia presents with 2 days of generalized fatigue and occasional lightheadedness. She denies fever, chest pain, dyspnea, or syncope. Her home blood glucose monitoring has been within her usual range. No recent changes in medication or diet.
physician_note: A: 74yo F with T2DM, hyperlipidemia, presenting with non-specific fatigue and lightheadedness. Vitals are stable, physical exam is benign, and initial workup including EKG and POC glucose is unremarkable. Differential includes mild dehydration, viral prodrome, or medication side effect. P: Provided oral rehydration. Will observe for symptom resolution. If stable, discharge home with strict return precautions and follow-up with PCP.

Name: beth arNOLd
chief_complaint: Severe chest pain and shortness of breath
hpi: A 77-year-old female with a history of diabetes presents with acute onset severe substernal chest pain radiating to the left arm, associated with dyspnea and diaphoresis. Symptoms started approximately 2 hours ago and have been worsening. She denies recent trauma or fever, but reports feeling generally unwell for the past day. She is currently on aspirin.
physician_note: ASSESSMENT: 77-year-old female presenting with acute coronary syndrome (ACS) symptoms. Differential includes myocardial infarction, unstable angina, aortic dissection, and pulmonary embolism. Her diabetes and age place her at high risk for cardiac events. PLAN: Initiate cardiac workup including serial ECGs, troponins, and chest X-ray. Administer aspirin, nitroglycerin, and oxygen as indicated. Monitor vital signs closely and prepare for possible cardiology consultation and cath lab activation.

Name: LiSa ThOrnTon
chief_complaint: Feeling weak and dizzy, blood sugar feels off
hpi: 42-year-old female with a history of type 2 diabetes mellitus presents with a two-day history of generalized weakness and intermittent dizziness. She reports adherence to her metformin and Lipitor but notes feeling more fatigued than usual. She denies chest pain, shortness of breath, or focal neurological deficits, and has been eating and drinking less due to mild nausea.
physician_note: A/P: 42F with T2DM presenting with weakness and dizziness. Differential includes mild hyperglycemia, dehydration, or a viral prodrome. Initial workup will include CBC, CMP, and urinalysis to assess for electrolyte abnormalities, infection, or significant glycemic derangement. Patient will be rehydrated with IV fluids and monitored for symptom improvement. If labs are reassuring and symptoms resolve, she will be discharged with return precautions and instructions for diabetic management.

Name: PhYLLis kLeiN
chief_complaint: Feeling dizzy and weak all day
hpi: A 67-year-old female with a history of hypertension presents with sudden onset dizziness and generalized weakness, starting approximately 6 hours ago. She reports feeling unsteady on her feet and experienced mild nausea, but denies vomiting, chest pain, or syncope. She is currently taking Lipitor and states she has been compliant with her medications.
physician_note: A 67F with HTN presents with acute dizziness and weakness. Differential diagnoses include orthostatic hypotension, transient ischemic attack, medication side effect, or benign positional vertigo. Plan includes IV hydration, comprehensive metabolic panel, CBC, EKG, and close neurological monitoring. Will consider CT head if symptoms persist or worsen after initial interventions.

Name: dENiSE GOoDWIN
chief_complaint: Worried about high blood sugar, feeling tired
hpi: 15-year-old male with Type 1 Diabetes presents to the ED with a chief complaint of feeling more fatigued than usual over the past 24 hours. He reports a home glucometer reading of 320 mg/dL earlier this morning, prompting the visit. He denies polyuria, polydipsia, abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, or Kussmaul respirations.
physician_note: A&P: 15 y/o male with T1DM presenting with mild fatigue and reported hyperglycemia. Current blood glucose is 190 mg/dL. No signs of DKA or other acute complications. Differential includes mild hyperglycemia secondary to dietary indiscretion or minor insulin dosing error. Plan: Monitor blood glucose, encourage oral hydration, reinforce sick day management, and discharge home with close follow-up with endocrinology.

scenario_hint: 79yo F vague chest discomfort + nausea + fatigue (atypical ACS presentation in elderly)
chief_complaint: Vague chest discomfort, nausea, and fatigue
hpi: 79-year-old female with a history of hypertension presents with several days of vague, non-exertional chest discomfort, intermittent nausea, and increasing fatigue. She reports taking aspirin daily. The patient denies diaphoresis, shortness of breath, or radiation of pain, but states she 'just doesn't feel right.'
physician_note: A&P: 79yo female with atypical chest pain symptoms concerning for an acute coronary syndrome given age, comorbidities (HTN), and non-specific presentation. Differential diagnosis includes ACS, GERD, anxiety, and musculoskeletal pain. Plan: ECG, serial troponins, CXR, comprehensive metabolic panel, CBC, IV access, cardiac monitoring, and aspirin 325mg chew.

Name: LISa DiXOn
chief_complaint: Severe right knee pain, swelling, and fever
hpi: A 20-year-old male with a history of arthritis presents with a 2-day history of acute onset severe right knee pain, swelling, and redness. He reports associated subjective fevers, chills, and generalized malaise. Denies recent trauma or new medications.
physician_note: This 20 y.o. male with known arthritis presents with acute monoarthritis of the right knee. Differential includes septic arthritis, acute inflammatory flare of underlying arthritis, or crystal-induced arthropathy. Initial labs are equivocal for infection, showing mild leukocytosis. Plan is to perform emergent arthrocentesis for synovial fluid analysis, initiate empiric antibiotics covering common pathogens, and obtain orthopedic consultation.

Name: aNgeL JOHNsON
chief_complaint: Dizziness and generalized weakness
hpi: Patient is a 72-year-old female with a history of hypertension, presenting with a 2-day history of progressive dizziness and generalized weakness. She reports lightheadedness upon standing but denies syncope or recent falls. No associated chest pain, shortness of breath, or focal neurological deficits. She is currently on Penicillin for an unrelated infection.
physician_note: A: 72F with chronic hypertension presenting with acute onset dizziness and weakness, suggestive of orthostatic intolerance. Differential includes dehydration, medication side effect, cardiac arrhythmia, or anemia. P: Will initiate IV fluid bolus, obtain 12-lead EKG, and order comprehensive labs including CBC, BMP, and cardiac enzymes. Monitor vital signs closely and consider adjusting current medications after further workup.

Name: eRIN gREEr
chief_complaint: Severe difficulty breathing and wheezing
hpi: A 29-year-old female with a history of asthma presents to the ED with acute shortness of breath, wheezing, and chest tightness for the past 6 hours. Symptoms started gradually and have worsened despite using her albuterol inhaler every 2 hours without significant relief. She denies fever or cough, but reports recent exposure to dust.
physician_note: A/P: Acute asthma exacerbation, likely triggered by environmental irritant. Differential includes bronchitis or early pneumonia. Plan: Nebulized albuterol/ipratropium, systemic corticosteroids, continuous SpO2 monitoring, CBC, CXR. Anticipate improvement with bronchodilators.

Name: JaSon MilLeR
chief_complaint: Generalized weakness and fever after chemotherapy
hpi: Patient is a 47-year-old female with a history of breast cancer currently undergoing chemotherapy. She presents with generalized weakness, chills, and a subjective fever for the past 24 hours, noting her last chemo session was 3 days ago. She denies any cough, dysuria, or focal pain, but feels profoundly unwell.
physician_note: This 47-year-old female, known for breast cancer, presents with suspected febrile neutropenia post-chemotherapy. Differential includes sepsis, viral syndrome, or chemotherapy-related adverse reaction. Plan includes immediate pan-culture, initiation of broad-spectrum antibiotics, IV fluid resuscitation, and admission for close monitoring and further workup.

scenario_hint: Severe community-acquired pneumonia with sepsis: high fever, productive cough, hypoxia
chief_complaint: Severe shortness of breath, cough, and high fever
hpi: A 67-year-old male with a history of asthma presents with a 3-day history of worsening productive cough with yellow sputum, subjective fevers to 103F, and progressive shortness of breath. He reports increasing wheezing and generalized weakness, unable to perform usual activities. His asthma has been well-controlled previously, with no recent exacerbations.
physician_note: A/P: 67M presenting with severe community-acquired pneumonia with sepsis, likely bacterial given sputum characteristics and high fevers. Differential diagnoses include acute asthma exacerbation, congestive heart failure, and pulmonary embolism. Plan: Admit to ICU for close monitoring, initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics (e.g., ceftriaxone and azithromycin), provide aggressive oxygen support via high-flow nasal cannula, administer IV fluids for hypotension, and obtain further blood cultures and a chest X-ray.

Name: JessICA CHuNG
chief_complaint: Teenager with sudden chest pain
hpi: A 13-year-old obese male presents with acute onset substernal chest pain, described as sharp and non-radiating, 7/10 in intensity, starting 2 hours prior. He denies recent trauma or fever. Parents report he is on daily low-dose aspirin, prescribed by an outside physician for an unspecified reason.
physician_note: 13 yo obese male presenting with acute chest pain. Differential includes musculoskeletal strain, esophagitis, anxiety, but cardiac etiology must be ruled out given the atypical presentation for age and aspirin use. Plan: EKG, cardiac enzymes, CXR, and observation. Will trend troponins and consult pediatric cardiology if indicated.

Name: mrs. maRIa nEwTon
chief_complaint: Feeling very tired and weak for days
hpi: A 33-year-old female with a history of cancer presents with progressive fatigue and generalized weakness over the past week. She reports decreased oral intake and occasional nausea but denies vomiting, fever, chills, or any focal neurological deficits. No recent chemotherapy or radiation.
physician_note: Pt is a 33 y.o. F with known cancer presenting with chronic fatigue and mild malaise. Vitals are stable and physical exam is non-focal. Differential includes anemia, electrolyte imbalance, or progression/recurrence of underlying malignancy. Given stable vitals and normal labs, plan for symptomatic management with IV fluids, antiemetics as needed, and close outpatient follow-up with oncology for further workup.

Name: SArah MENdOZa
chief_complaint: Feeling very weak and tired for a few days
hpi: A 73-year-old female with a history of breast cancer (status post chemotherapy, last cycle 3 weeks ago) presents with progressive generalized weakness and fatigue for the past 3 days. She reports reduced oral intake and has been taking ibuprofen for generalized body aches without significant relief. No fever, chills, cough, or chest pain. She denies any new focal neurological deficits.
physician_note: A&P: 73 y.o. female with breast cancer s/p chemotherapy presenting with generalized weakness and decreased oral intake. Vitals are stable, and physical exam is largely benign, showing only mild dehydration. Differential diagnosis includes post-chemotherapy fatigue, dehydration, electrolyte derangement, or occult infection. Plan: Order CBC, CMP, urinalysis; administer IV fluids for rehydration; monitor symptoms, and consider oncology consultation pending lab results.

Name: DONNa aLlen
chief_complaint: Severe headache and neck pain
hpi: 31-year-old female with a history of hypertension presents with a severe, throbbing headache for the past two days, accompanied by neck stiffness. She reports taking her usual Paracetamol without significant relief. Denies fever, vision changes, focal weakness, or recent trauma.
physician_note: Assessment: Headache, likely tension-type or benign exertional given current stable vitals and absence of red flags. Differential includes mild hypertensive urgency given history, though current BP not critically elevated. Plan: Administer IV fluids and NSAIDs for symptom control. Monitor blood pressure closely. If symptoms improve and neurological exam remains benign, discharge with advice for close follow-up with PCP for hypertension management.

Name: RIcHaRd rObiNSoN
chief_complaint: Fell at home, feeling dizzy and weak.
hpi: An 80-year-old female with obesity presents to the ED after a syncopal episode at home. Patient reports feeling dizzy for the past day, followed by a brief loss of consciousness while standing up from a chair. She denies head trauma but endorsed generalized weakness and lightheadedness prior to the fall.
physician_note: A&P: 80yo F with obesity presenting after syncopal episode. Vitals are stable, physical exam unremarkable aside from mild orthostatic changes. Differential includes orthostatic hypotension, dehydration, vasovagal syncope, or less likely, a cardiac arrhythmia given normal ECG. Plan: IV fluids, orthostatic vital signs monitoring, basic labs, and consider telemetry if symptoms persist. Patient will be discharged once stable with fall precautions education.

Name: VINCenT EAtOn
chief_complaint: Abdominal pain and vomiting with diabetes
hpi: 17 y.o. male with Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus presenting with a 1-day history of severe abdominal pain, nausea, and multiple episodes of emesis. Patient reports poor oral intake and admits to missing insulin doses due to feeling sick. Denies fever, chest pain, or dysuria.
physician_note: Assessment: 17 y.o. male with T1DM presenting with diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA) symptoms. Differential includes DKA, gastroenteritis, and appendicitis. Plan: Initiate IV fluids, insulin drip, and obtain labs including VBG, electrolytes, renal function, and urinalysis. Closely monitor glucose and ketone levels.

Name: StACY MOSES
chief_complaint: Difficulty breathing, asthma flare-up
hpi: A 29-year-old male with a history of asthma presents to the ED with acute onset dyspnea and wheezing for the past 6 hours. He reports worsening symptoms despite frequent home albuterol use. No associated fever, cough, or recent illness. He denies chest pain or syncope.
physician_note: A/P: 29M presenting with acute asthma exacerbation. Differential diagnosis includes viral bronchitis or allergic reaction. Plan: Initiate nebulized albuterol/ipratropium, administer systemic corticosteroids, and provide oxygen support as needed. Monitor respiratory status closely for improvement or worsening of symptoms. Will consider magnesium sulfate if poor response to initial therapy.

scenario_hint: DKA: blood glucose 580, anion gap 24, vomiting, Kussmaul breathing
chief_complaint: Feeling really sick, throwing up, can't catch my breath
hpi: A 28-year-old male with Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus on insulin presents with a 2-day history of nausea, vomiting, and polyuria. He reports missing several insulin doses recently. Patient now complains of generalized weakness and deep, rapid breathing.
physician_note: A/P: 28M presenting in diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA), likely secondary to insulin non-adherence. Initial labs significant for severe hyperglycemia and metabolic acidosis. Plan: Aggressive intravenous fluid resuscitation, insulin drip initiation, and electrolyte repletion with close monitoring for cerebral edema. DDx includes DKA, hyperosmolar hyperglycemic state (HHS), and severe gastroenteritis.

Name: MaUrIce CARtER
chief_complaint: Severe stomach pain and feeling weak
hpi: A 14-year-old male with a history of obesity presents to the ED with 12 hours of worsening epigastric pain, radiating to his back. He reports taking over-the-counter Aspirin daily for chronic knee pain for the past week. Associated symptoms include nausea, one episode of non-bloody emesis, and generalized fatigue.
physician_note: A: 14yo male with obesity presenting with acute epigastric pain concerning for gastritis/PUD vs. pancreatitis vs. early appendicitis. Aspirin use is a significant factor. P: Labs (CBC, LFTs, Lipase, UA), IV fluids, PPI, pain control. Monitor for GI bleeding. Consult Surgery if appendicitis cannot be ruled out or if symptoms worsen.

Name: eRIc CaRTEr
chief_complaint: Acute severe knee pain and fever
hpi: A 26-year-old male with a history of seronegative arthritis presents with a 2-day history of acute onset severe right knee pain, swelling, and redness. He reports associated subjective fevers and chills. Patient states his usual Ibuprofen regimen has not provided relief, and the pain is significantly worse than his typical arthritis flares. He denies any recent trauma or skin breaks near the joint.
physician_note: 26M with known arthritis presenting with acute monoarticular arthritis and systemic symptoms concerning for septic joint. Differential diagnoses include septic arthritis, acute gout flare, or reactive arthritis. Plan to obtain arthrocentesis for synovial fluid analysis, initiate empiric broad-spectrum antibiotics, and provide aggressive pain control. Will also order inflammatory markers and blood cultures.

Name: AndREw garCia
chief_complaint: Severe shortness of breath, cannot catch breath
hpi: 53-year-old female with a history of asthma presents to the ED with acute onset of severe shortness of breath that started approximately 2 hours ago. She reports worsening wheezing and cough, unresponsive to her home albuterol inhaler. Denies fever, chest pain, or recent illness. Patient states this is worse than her usual exacerbations.
physician_note: A: 53 y.o. female with acute asthma exacerbation. Differential includes viral bronchitis, early pneumonia, or reactive airway disease. P: Administer nebulized albuterol/ipratropium, systemic corticosteroids (prednisone), and supplemental oxygen to maintain SpO2 >92%. Monitor respiratory status closely, consider chest X-ray if no improvement or signs of infection. Will reassess in 1 hour.

Name: jENNifER CUnNingham
chief_complaint: Feeling weak and tired
hpi: A 79-year-old female with a history of metastatic colon cancer, currently on chemotherapy, presents to the ED with progressively worsening generalized weakness and fatigue for the past week. She reports decreased oral intake and occasional lightheadedness upon standing. She denies fever, chills, chest pain, or focal neurological deficits. She is on Lipitor.
physician_note: 79F with known metastatic colon cancer presenting with generalized weakness and fatigue. Differential includes cancer-related fatigue, dehydration, electrolyte disturbance, or potential progression of disease. Given stable vitals and initial normal labs, plan to administer IV fluids, obtain further labs including comprehensive metabolic panel, and consult with oncology. Patient to be admitted for observation and workup.

Name: aLan ShARp
chief_complaint: Weakness, dizziness, and trouble breathing
hpi: An 83-year-old obese male presents with a 2-day history of increasing generalized weakness, fatigue, and occasional dizziness upon standing. He reports mild exertional dyspnea but denies chest pain or fever at home. His wife notes he has been less active and somewhat confused compared to his baseline.
physician_note: 83 yo M with acute onset weakness, dyspnea, and mild confusion. Differential includes UTI, early sepsis, cardiac arrhythmia/decompensation, or dehydration. Plan includes IV fluids, full septic workup (UA, blood cultures, CXR), EKG, and comprehensive metabolic panel. Monitor vitals closely for any deterioration.

Name: kAthleEn shIElDs
chief_complaint: Chest discomfort and feeling weak.
hpi: 81-year-old female with history of hypertension, on Lipitor, presents with one day of generalized weakness and intermittent, non-radiating chest discomfort, described as a dull ache. She denies dyspnea, palpitations, syncope, or fever. Symptoms are worse with exertion but resolve with rest. Patient reports feeling generally unwell since yesterday morning.
physician_note: A&P: 81F presenting with atypical chest pain and weakness. Differential includes NSTEMI, unstable angina, GERD, and musculoskeletal pain given her age and comorbidities. Initial EKG shows non-specific ST-T changes and a preliminary troponin is borderline elevated. Plan: Admit for cardiac workup, serial troponins, continuous EKG monitoring, and cardiology consultation.

Name: KaReN sanTANA
chief_complaint: Chest pain and shortness of breath
hpi: Patient is a 56-year-old male with a history of hypertension presenting with acute onset substernal chest pain for approximately 4 hours. The pain is described as pressure, radiating to the left arm, and associated with mild shortness of breath. He reports no aggravating or alleviating factors and took Paracetamol at home without relief prior to arrival.
physician_note: 56 y/o male with new onset chest pain concerning for acute coronary syndrome. Differential also includes pericarditis, aortic dissection, and esophageal etiology. Initial ECG shows non-specific T-wave changes, and troponin is borderline elevated. Plan: serial troponins, repeat ECG, cardiac enzymes, CXR, and cardiology consultation for further evaluation and management.

Name: kENNEth MUrRAY
chief_complaint: Weakness and dizziness, 82-year-old diabetic female
hpi: Patient is an 82-year-old female with a history of T2DM presenting with generalized weakness and intermittent dizziness for the past 2 days. She reports decreased oral intake and occasional lightheadedness upon standing. No chest pain, shortness of breath, fever, or focal neurological deficits. Denies recent illness or trauma. Last dose of paracetamol was yesterday for mild body aches.
physician_note: 82F T2DM presenting with acute onset generalized weakness and dizziness, likely multifactorial. Differential includes dehydration, poorly controlled diabetes, or early infection. Vitals stable but patient appears fatigued. Plan: IV fluids, full lab workup including CBC, CMP, and urinalysis. Monitor glucose, consider admission for further workup and hydration.

Name: cyNThIA bRadLEy
chief_complaint: Bad stomach ache getting worse
hpi: A 78-year-old obese male presents with worsening lower abdominal pain for the past 2 days. The pain is described as dull and crampy, without radiation, and has not improved with his usual ibuprofen. He denies fever, nausea, vomiting, or diarrhea, but reports some constipation recently.
physician_note: 78 y.o. male with chronic abdominal pain now acutely worsened. Differential includes diverticulitis, gastritis, or potential bowel obstruction given constipation. Plan includes CBC, CMP, lipase, urinalysis, and CT abdomen/pelvis to evaluate for acute pathology. Will monitor pain and admit if imaging is positive or symptoms persist.

Name: caMEroN martInez
chief_complaint: Sudden confusion and weakness
